title: True or False? — Daily Routine sentences
subtitle: 10 sentences about a typical morning. Read carefully and decide (Track A-B · Basic-Intermediate)

items.text: "I always wake up at 7" means I wake up at 7 every day.
items.explanation: Correct. "Always" = every time, with no exceptions. Position: before the main verb (wake up).

items.text: "She have breakfast" is correct in Present Simple.
items.explanation: Wrong. In 3rd person (he/she/it) we add -s: "She has breakfast". The verb "have" becomes "has".

items.text: Frequency adverbs go BEFORE the verb "to be".
items.explanation: Wrong. With "to be": adverb comes AFTER. Example: "He is always late" (not "He always is late"). With other verbs: BEFORE.
Correct. Time expressions with day-units use "on": on Monday, on weekends, on my birthday.

items.text: "I never eat fish" is grammatically correct.
items.explanation: Correct. "Never" is a frequency adverb meaning 0% of the time. Position: before the main verb.

items.text: "At the morning" is the correct time expression.
items.explanation: Wrong. The correct chunk is "in the morning" — we use "in" with parts of the day (in the morning, in the afternoon, in the evening). But "AT night".

items.text: "I usually goes to bed at 10" is correct.
items.explanation: Wrong. With "I" (1st person) we don't add -s. The correct form is "I usually go to bed".

items.text: "Every day" can replace "always" in many sentences.
items.explanation: Correct. "I always brush my teeth" ≈ "I brush my teeth every day". Both express 100% frequency.

items.text: Past Simple is the same as Present Simple — just add -s.
items.explanation: Wrong. Past Simple uses -ed (played, walked) or irregular forms (went, came). Present Simple uses -s only in 3rd person.

items.text: "What time do you wake up?" is a correct question.
items.explanation: Correct. Question word + auxiliary "do" + subject + main verb. We use "do" with "you/I/we/they" and "does" with "he/she/it".
